mission.title: Install and Configure Composefs on Kubernetes
mission.description: Composefs provides a flexible mechanism to support read-only mountable filesystem trees, combining the reliability of disk images with the flexibility of files. Installing Composefs allows you to efficiently manage filesystem images while avoiding common drawbacks associated with traditional disk images.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Composefs to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy Composefs Components
steps.description: Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Composefs.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the Composefs pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Composefs pods running in the 'composefs' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine. Resource limits will be applied to ensure optimal performance.

uninstall.title: Remove Composefs Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the deployed resources and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Composefs
upgrade.description: To upgrade Composefs, re-apply the latest manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n composefs`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the service is correctly defined and check the service endpoints with: `kubectl get endpoints -n composefs`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are pending, verify the resource requests and limits in the deployment manifest and adjust them accordingly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to communicate with it.
